response: The global food system faces mounting challenges as the population is projected to hit 9.7 billion by 2050, demanding a 60% boost in food production amid climate change, water depletion, and degradation of 33% of arable land. Agriculture itself drives about 25% of greenhouse gas emissions, while roughly one-third of food produced is wasted. Proposed solutions include precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant GMOs, regenerative practices, and policy reforms on subsidies and carbon pricing. The central challenge is scaling these innovations while keeping food affordable and accessible to the world's poorest.
